direction_gloss.D1: genotype-present, phenotype-absent (canonical causal variant present, phenotype absent - reduced penetrance; modifier nodes block the path)
direction_gloss.D2: phenotype-present, genotype-absent (phenotype without the canonical variant - an alternative gene/route reaches the endpoint)
direction_gloss.both: both D1 and D2 evidence catalogued for this gene
direction_gloss.mixed: direction varies across the papers citing this gene

caveats: OCA2 has a single backbone edge (MITF->OCA2); HERC2 is not a backbone node. D1/D2 are NOT computed by graph traversal - they are the pre-registered, cited case classification. The network panel DISPLAYS the subgraph as mechanistic context, it does not derive direction.
rs12913832 and rs1800401 positions are in DIFFERENT genome builds (GRCh38 vs GRCh37); display the shared cytoband 15q13.1 rather than bare bp, or liftover first.

rs12913832 was NOT genome-wide significant in the Ang2023 Kalinago cohort (P_adj=0.075); the HERC2->OCA2 enhancer mechanism is the cited external finding (PMID:22234890, Visser 2012), not a Kalinago result.

examples.title: rs12913832: HERC2-labelled, OCA2-regulating (eye colour)
examples.thesis: The SNP's nearest-gene label (HERC2) is not its functional target (OCA2). A naive nearest-gene tool stops at HERC2; the cited regulatory evidence redirects to OCA2, which is in the mechanistic network.

locus.traits: eye colour; skin pigmentation; hair colour; suntan; cutaneous melanoma; actinic keratosis

annotation_edges.evidence_citation: GWAS Catalog mapped_gene (nearest gene by position); dbSNP rs12913832
annotation_edges.notes: Intron variant within HERC2; nearest-gene label, NOT the functional target.

annotation_edges.evidence_citation: PMID:22234890 (Visser, Kayser & Palstra 2012, Genome Res 22:446-455, doi:10.1101/gr.128652.111)
annotation_edges.notes: rs12913832 sits in a HERC2 intron ~21 kb from OCA2; allele attenuates a long-range enhancer / chromatin loop regulating OCA2 transcription. Established in forensic/European pigmentation literature; in the Ang2023 Kalinago cohort this SNP was NOT genome-wide significant (P_adj=0.075) - the regulatory mechanism is the cited external finding, not a Kalinago result.

direction.label: mixed
direction.gloss: direction varies across the papers citing this gene
direction.detail: D1 illustration in this locus: Pospiech2016 reports the CC (blue-associated) genotype is incompletely penetrant - a fraction of CC individuals are NOT blue-eyed (genotype-present, phenotype-absent).
direction.citation: Pospiech2016 Int J Legal Med (IrisPlex population); OCA2 direction from case classification

case_text.association: Univariate OR 0.031 (0.021–0.045), p=1.961×10−72, Nagelkerke R2=55.6%; Multivariate OR 0.020 (0.013–0.032), p=9.055×10−64. Correlation: 0.566/0.766 (Large), Pearson p=5.711×10−138 [4-cat]; 0.698/0.810 (Large), p=1.189×10−108 [blue/non-blue]. C allele increases odds of blue eye colour by factor 32.3; strongest predictor, rank 1
case_text.penetrance_gap: 14.3% of CC (blue-associated) males are NOT blue-eyed. CC blue proportion M 85.7% vs F 77.0%, p=0.007 (gender difference within CC)

examples.title: Kalinago OCA2: R305W (D1) and NW273KV (D2) in one case (Ang 2023)
examples.thesis: Both discordance directions appear at one in-network gene. R305W is correctly OCA2-labelled and predicted-pathogenic yet non-penetrant (D1). The albino individuals carry no catalogued OCA2 variant, only the novel NW273KV a standard panel would miss (D2).

locus.source_citation: Ang2023 eLife 10.7554/eLife.77514 (position GRCh37/b37 as reported); dbSNP rs1800401

locus_secondary.source_citation: Ang2023 eLife 10.7554/eLife.77514 (exon 8 MNP c.819_822delCTGGinsGGTC); dbSNP rs797044784

annotation_edges.notes: OCA2 R305W missense; positional label == functional gene here (coding variant in OCA2). Scores as pathogenic by SIFT/PolyPhen2/PANTHER (CADD 22.7) yet shows no detectable pigmentation effect once NW273KV is controlled (P_adj=0.981). This is the D1 anchor (genotype-present, phenotype-absent): predicted-pathogenic variant, phenotype absent.

annotation_edges.evidence_citation: Ang2023 eLife 10.7554/eLife.77514 (Table 2, exon 8 MNP); dbSNP rs797044784
annotation_edges.notes: OCA2 NW273KV novel multi-nucleotide albinism allele; causal in Kalinago (per-allele -7.7 melanin-index units, p=2.2e-5). This is the D2 counterpart (phenotype-present, genotype-absent for the CATALOGUED variants): albinism traces to a population-specific novel variant a standard OCA2 panel would miss.

direction.label: both
direction.gloss: both D1 and D2 evidence catalogued for this gene
direction.gene_level_note: OCA2's direction ACROSS ALL citing papers is 'mixed'; WITHIN Ang2023 specifically it is 'both' (D1 via R305W + D2 via NW273KV). Complementary, not contradictory.
direction.detail: D1 = R305W predicted-pathogenic yet no effect once NW273KV controlled. D2 = albinism traces to the novel NW273KV, not any catalogued OCA2 variant.

case_text.R305W_D1: NOT causal for skin color; scores INCORRECTLY as pathogenic by SIFT/Polyphen2.0/PANTHER; no detectable effect when controlling for NW273KV. Freq 0.068 (b37 pos 28260053). CADD 22.7
case_text.NW273KV_D2: CAUSAL for albinism. Per-allele (1st allele) effect -7.7 MI (p=2.2E-5, Table2); -7.744 MI (p=9.0E-7, App1-T5). Freq 0.03. HW albinism freq ~1/1000 matches observed 3/~3000
